Golam Azom, Ph.D.

Postdoctoral Associate

Department of Mechanical Engineering

Binghamton University

azom.chem.lsu@gmail.com

I am a computational materials scientist and currently a Postdoctoral Associate in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Binghamton University. My research focuses on atomistic modeling to investigate complex interfacial processes, chemical reactivity relevant to environmental remediation, separation science, and energy storage systems. I also study transport phenomena in nanofluidic environments.

My work combines quantum mechanical methods, molecular simulations, machine-learned force fields (MLFFs), and generative AI/LLM-driven workflows to accelerate materials discovery, with a growing focus on semiconductor manufacturing and advanced materials design.

I earned my PhD in Chemistry from Louisiana State University and my B.Sc. in Applied Chemistry and Chemical Engineering from University of Dhaka.

Solid-Liquid Interfaces Machine-Learning Force Fields Nanofluidics Separation Science Agentic AI Energy and Fuels Semiconductors
